title: gypsophila muralis subsp. repens
intro: Gypsophila muralis subsp. repens, also known as creeping baby's breath, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Gypsophila muralis subsp. repens include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and promoting relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
